_note: This file is a design-time snapshot and may be out of date. For current step state, run: iw item-status <ID> --json. The DB is the authoritative source of truth (CR-00023).
title: Daemon marks code-review step as PID-dead when reviewer exits without iw step-done; downstream review chain loops unboundedly

steps.description: Backend: add _try_recover_completed_review_step(run) helper in orch/daemon/step_monitor.py. When pid_alive=False AND _probe_for_child=False AND run.step_type in ('code_review','code_review_final'), look for ai-dev/active/<ITEM>/reports/<ITEM>_<STEP>_*_report.md with mtime > run.started_at. If found, parse the JSON contract block (verdict, findings, mandatory_fix_count) and write completion via the same path iw step-done uses. Transition the step to completed (or needs_fix on verdict=fail). Emit DaemonEvent type='step_run_recovered_from_report'. Add structured INFO logging. If no report exists, fall through to _handle_crashed unchanged. Touch ONLY orch/daemon/step_monitor.py.

Per-agent review of S01: report-file glob is anchored at item+step (no cross-item contamination); JSON parse is robust to missing/malformed fields (returns None → falls through to _handle_crashed); non-code-review step types are unchanged; structured logging captures the recovery decision (report path, mtime, verdict); pytest-randomly compatibility; no new external deps.

Backend: add cumulative per-work-item review-relaunch cap to orch/daemon/fix_cycle.py and orch/daemon/batch_manager.py. New env var IW_CORE_MAX_REVIEW_RELAUNCHES_PER_ITEM (default 15). Counter is computed from step_runs (not in-memory) so it survives daemon restart. When exceeded, transition the work item to status='failed' and emit DaemonEvent type='review_relaunch_cap_exceeded' carrying the cap, the actual count, and the list of relaunch tuples. Touch ONLY orch/daemon/fix_cycle.py and orch/daemon/batch_manager.py.

Per-agent review of S03: cap read from env once at module load with explicit default; counter persists across daemon restart (computed from step_runs, not in-memory); emit-failed path is idempotent; no race with concurrent fix-cycle completions; DaemonEvent payload includes the diagnostic tuples; no scope creep outside the two named files.

Reviewer-prompt diff scoping. Modify agents/code-review-impl.md AND commands/code-review-impl.md so the reviewer is instructed to restrict its diff to files matching the step's scope.allowed_paths globs from workflow-manifest.json — NOT 'git diff HEAD'. Update skills/iw-workflow/SKILL.md to document the convention. Do NOT modify any daemon code in this step. Touch ONLY agents/code-review-impl.md, commands/code-review-impl.md, and skills/iw-workflow/SKILL.md.

Per-agent review of S05: prompt change applied to BOTH master copies (agents/code-review-impl.md and commands/code-review-impl.md); SKILL.md update is congruent; no contradictory 'git diff HEAD' phrasing remains anywhere in the master review prompt; allowed_paths is correctly identified as the authoritative scope source (matches worktree_commit.sh Step 2.25 enforcement).

Tests: CREATE (a) tests/unit/daemon/test_step_monitor_i00116_review_recovery.py with the three tests verbatim from the design's 'Test to Reproduce' section; (b) tests/integration/test_fix_cycle_review_relaunch_cap.py exercising the cap with a real db_session — under-cap path unchanged, over-cap path transitions item to failed with the expected DaemonEvent; (c) tests/unit/test_review_prompt_scope.py asserting the master prompt files reference allowed_paths AND do NOT contain unbounded 'git diff HEAD' guidance. ALL assertions must be SEMANTIC (specific verdict values, specific DaemonEvent types, specific cap counts) — never shape-only. Tests must be order-independent (pytest-randomly). Targeted verification only: uv run pytest tests/unit/daemon/test_step_monitor_i00116_review_recovery.py tests/integration/test_fix_cycle_review_relaunch_cap.py tests/unit/test_review_prompt_scope.py -v. Do NOT run make test-unit / make test-integration. Do NOT touch any production code.

Per-agent review of S07: all three test files present and named exactly; assertions are semantic (specific values + structured locations); cap test uses a real db_session not a MagicMock (FOR UPDATE locking must be exercised — see tests/CLAUDE.md rule 3); pytest-randomly compatibility (order-independent); no production code touched.

Global cross-agent review of S01/S03/S05/S07: AC1 (review steps with on-disk reports recovered) demonstrated by test_i00116_review_step_with_report_on_disk_is_recovered_not_crashed + step_monitor recovery helper; AC2 (review steps without reports still crash) demonstrated by test_i00116_review_step_without_report_still_marked_crashed; AC3 (cap breaks loops) demonstrated by tests/integration/test_fix_cycle_review_relaunch_cap.py + fix_cycle/batch_manager logic; AC4 (prompt scope) demonstrated by tests/unit/test_review_prompt_scope.py + agents/commands/SKILL.md changes; AC5 (regression tests exist) by all three test files green. Cross-layer scope adherence (only files in scope.allowed_paths are touched). New env var IW_CORE_MAX_REVIEW_RELAUNCHES_PER_ITEM is documented in CLAUDE.md (or docs/IW_AI_Core_Daemon_Design.md) — add a note if missing.
Self-assessment of the just-completed item via the iw-item-analyze skill
